spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates a real authoring use case: split a glob on wildcard tokens, escape only the literal segments through `RegExp.escape`, then re-join. The built regex is shown live and run against editable sample paths.

interactive.rationale: Live updates on glob input, paths textarea, and two checkboxes (case-insensitive, anchor-end); built-regex readout updates on every keystroke and each path gets a pass/fail row.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index says `keeping wildcards alive while escaping the literal path segments. Try paths with parens, dots, and dollars` — the sample paths include `(group)`, `$weird`, and `a+b` exactly to that script.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers a path with parens/dots/dollars, but never shows what happens when the user enters a glob with no wildcards (pure literal) or one with a leading glob (`**/Card.tsx`) where the order of literal-vs-token matters. Anchor toggle exists but is never explained against a path that would only match without anchoring.

references.rationale: The `see also` section lists only sibling concept pages — it does NOT include the chromestatus.com/feature/5179154657705984 link, which CLAUDE.md flags as a per-page invariant (#3) for self-heal.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables, no raw hex, no button rules that strand color from background.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md invariant #3: every page must include the `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5179154657705984` link in references so the slug-recovery cleanup script can self-heal. This concept page omits it and only links siblings. Add the chromestatus entry (and ideally the TC39 proposal) to `see also`.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the pure-literal and leading-wildcard cases
openQuestions.detail: The current sample is `src/**/components/*.tsx`. Add presets for (a) a literal glob with no wildcards — e.g. `src/index.ts` — to show the wildcard-free path through `RegExp.escape`, and (b) a leading-wildcard glob like `**/Card.tsx` to exercise the empty-prefix branch where `parts[0]` is the empty string before the first token.

openQuestions.title: Surface what the anchor-end toggle does in practice
openQuestions.detail: The `match full path` checkbox flips `$` on/off but the live regex preview is the only feedback. Add a worked example sample path (`src/app/components/Button.tsx.bak`) that only matches when anchoring is off, so the toggle visibly does something.

openQuestions.title: Add a `?` single-char wildcard example
openQuestions.detail: The code handles `?` -> `[^/]` but no sample uses it. Either remove the `?` case from the code (it's dead) or add a sample glob that uses it (`*.ts?`) to keep the API surface honest.

summary: Useful authoring-tool demo that hits a real day-to-day glob-to-regex problem and visibly leans on `RegExp.escape` for the literal segments. The page is well-built but breaks the project invariant of always linking back to chromestatus, and the test paths cover only the middle case — a literal-only glob and a leading-wildcard glob would round it out.
